text: This is a test process for this reactor.
First, I will describe the gas feed conditions.
The anode feed gas is hydrogen with a flow rate of 20 ccm.
The cathode feed gas is carbon monoxide with a flow rate of 10 ccm.
In the electrochemical setup, the working electrode is connected to the cathode,
and both the counter electrode and the reference electrode are connected at the same time.
During the test, the middle chamber is filled with a liquid electrolyte.
The liquid electrolyte is 1 molar KOH.
Different from the previous reactor, where the middle chamber needs solid electrolyte,
this reactor uses a circulating liquid electrolyte in the middle chamber.
The purpose of this design is to improve ionic conductivity, reduce the resistance of the middle chamber,
and lower the overall cell voltage during operation.
The liquid electrolyte circulation rate is 30 ccm.
For this test, we will monitor the cell voltage and evaluate whether the circulating 1 molar KOH electrolyte
can improve the electrochemical performance and solubility of this reactor.
